answers: Active recall, spaced repetition, teach others, Pomodoro, quiet environment, past papers.
Study smarter: use active recall instead of re-reading, space out your revision sessions, and test yourself with past papers.
Best study techniques: Pomodoro (25 min on, 5 min off), teach what you learned to someone else, and review material at increasing intervals.
Top tip: close your notes and try to recall everything you just read — that's active recall, and it's the most powerful study method there is.
